channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Bus crashes into market in Kwara
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
A commercial bus lost its brakes and crashed into a market in Ilorin, Kwara State. Miraculously, no fatalities were recorded in the bus crash in Kwara.
Read More: https://punchng.com/bus-crashes-into-market-in-kwara/

item.title: UK warns young travellers against accepting ‘free holidays’ from drug gangs
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The UK Home Office warns young travellers against accepting ‘free holidays’ from drug gangs, urging caution to avoid severe penalties for drug smuggling.
Read More: https://punchng.com/uk-warns-young-travellers-against-accepting-free-holidays-from-drug-gangs/

item.title: UN chief urges global action against poverty on Nelson Mandela Day
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
On Nelson Mandela Day, the UN Secretary-General calls for global action to eradicate poverty and inequality, honouring Mandela’s legacy of justice.
Read More: https://punchng.com/un-chief-urges-global-action-against-poverty-on-nelson-mandela-day/

item.title: Outcry as Imo pupils sit for examinations in darkness after delays
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Outcry in Imo State as primary school pupils are forced to sit for First School Leaving Certificate and Common Entrance exams in darkness due to severe del
Read More: https://punchng.com/outcry-as-imo-pupils-sit-for-examinations-in-darkness-after-delays/

item.title: Lehmann: Langer would be 'really good' in England Test coach role
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Darren Lehmann revealed he has not been contacted about becoming England's next Test head coach, before endorsing fellow Australian Justin Langer for the vacant position.

item.title: Peter Obi visits Isaac Fayose, disowns online attackers [VIDEO]
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Presidential candidate of the Nigerian Democratic Congress, NDC, Peter Obi, on Saturday paid a visit to one of his prominent supporters, Isaac Fayose, in Abuja. The visit followed the controversy surrounding Fayose’s recent meeting with President Bola Tinubu’s son, Seyi Tinubu.

item.title: Army-Air Force Feud Crippled Counterterrorism Under Buhari – Presidency
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Presidency has alleged that a rivalry between the Nigerian Army and the Nigerian Air Force during former President Muhammadu Buhari’s administration undermined counterterrorism operations, with troops allegedly denied crucial air support during some military missions. Special adviser policy comm

item.title: World Cup Third-Place Play-Off Should Be Scrapped —Shearer
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Alan Shearer has branded FIFA’s third-place play-off ‘meaningless’ ahead of England’s meeting with France this evening. England’s defeat by Argentina in Wednesday’s semi-final ended their hopes of reaching Sunday’s World Cup final against Spain in New Jersey

item.title: Jigawa Launches Digital Tax Platform to Boost Revenue, End Leakages
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: The Jigawa State Government is taking another step to strengthen its economy with the launch of a digital tax platform aimed at improving revenue collection from the informal sector, ending leakages and making tax payments more transparent. item.title: NCC, NDLEA Sign MoU To Combat Copyright Piracy, Organised Crime
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Nigerian Copyright Commission (NCC) and the National Drug Law Enforcement Agency (NDLEA) have signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) to strengthen inter-agency cooperation in combating copyright piracy and related organised criminal activities. The MoU was signed yesterday, Friday, July 17,

item.title: Military rescues over 40 kidnapped victims, arrests 20 suspected criminals nationwide
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Nigerian military says its troops rescued more than 40 kidnapped victims and arrested over 20 suspected terrorists, collaborators and other criminals during operations carried out across the country between July 10 and 16. The Director of Defence Media Operations, Maj.-Gen. Michael Onoja, disclo

Monica Yahaya Kaltho, has officially withdrawn from the court case instituted by some aggrieved, non-cleared governorship aspirants challenging the outcome of the party’s governorship primary election. item.title: Ekiti court jails two men for roaming streets naked
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: An Ado-Ekiti Chief Magistrate Court has sentenced two men, Adeniyi Oluwole, 49, and Ogunlola John, 30, to one month imprisonment without the option of a fine for roaming the streets naked. The defendants were arraigned on a one-count charge of conduct likely to cause a breach of public peace, contra

item.dc:creator: Daily Post

The decision followed an unscheduled late-night inspection of the facility, during which the governor assessed the quality and pace of wor

item.title: Apo-Karshi road will be completed before end of 2026, Wike assures Tinubu
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Federal Capital Territory (FCT) Minister, Nyesom Wike, has assured President Bola Ahmed Tinubu that the long-abandoned 13-kilometre Apo-Karshi Road will be completed before the end of this year, 2026. The minister gave the assurance while commissioning the reconstructed Arab/Gbazango Road in Kubwa,

item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: On July 23, Nigeria’s main grassroots tournament kicks off: players from the top 100 teams across five divisions of Lagos will compete for the championship title, a generous prize pool, the prestige of their home communities, and their dream of signing their first contract with a professional club.

item.title: Three siblings reportedly kidnapped while transporting mother’s corpse for burial in Benue
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Three siblings travelling from Abuja to Benue State to bury their late mother have reportedly been abducted by suspected gunmen along the Ogobia–Otukpo Road. The incident was said to have occurred in the early hours of Saturday. It was brought to public attention by citizen journalist Meddy Olo

item.title: Pfizer Paid Out $14.6 Billion in Dividends Over the Last 18 Months. Can It Keep This Up Through the Patent Cliff?
item.dc:creator: Yahoo Finance

item.title: Transfer: Trabzonspor slaps €30m price tag on Onuachu
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Trabzonspor have placed a €30 million price tag on Super Eagles striker Paul Onuachu, DAILY POST reports. Onuachu is a subject of interest from Turkish Super Lig giants Fenerbahçe, and Saudi club Al-Ahli. Another Turkish club Beşiktaş also have interest in the Nigeria international. Trabzonspor will

item.title: FUOYE SUG President Says Tiwa Savage’s Sex Tape Response Helped Him Overcome Leak
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: James Iyanuoluwa Adio, the suspended President of the Students’ Union Government (SUG) of the Federal University Oye-Ekiti (FUOYE), has revealed that the way Nigerian music star Tiwa Savage handled the leak of her private sex tape inspired him to overcome a similar experience. Adio made the di

item.title: Mutfwang Woos Manufacturers, Unveils Private Sector Roadmap For Plateau Industrial Growth
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: The Governor said his administration was determined to unlock the economic potential of agriculture, mining, tourism, and manufacturing to reduce the state's dependence on federal allocations. The post Mutfwang Woos Manufacturers, Unveils Private Sector Roadmap For Plateau Industrial Growth appeared

item.title: Nollywood police actor, Owolabi Ajasa debunks news of death [VIDEO]
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Owolabi Ajasa, a popular Nollywood police actor, known as ‘Ta lo pa chief’, has debunked the viral news of his own death. DAILY POST reports that the news about the actor’s death went viral after an obituary poster announcing his demise surfaced on social media. Reacting to the new

item.title: NCDC, UNICEF Partner Niger Govt On Disease Control
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: …As Niger records one death, 4 hospitalised Following the outbreak of suspected diphtheria cases in Niger State, the Nigeria Centre for Disease Control and Prevention (NCDC) has said the Centre will maintain sustained collaboration with the State Government to curb outbreaks and safeguard publ

item.title: Investors Propose $145m Solar Manufacturing Plant In Abia
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: ‎An indigenous firm, MD Nwakanma Nigeria Limited has proposed to establish a solar manufacturing plant in Abia State through a Public-Private Partnership (PPP) arrangement with the state government. ‎ ‎Team lead and representative of the investors, Oko Jaja stated this when they visited the governor

item.title: ‘I am at a loss’: My brother is dying. We are not close. What responsibility do I have to help him?
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: “He is now no longer able to think clearly or communicate his wishes.”

item.title: Kano to establish cottage industry hubs to boost local economy, create jobs
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Kano State Governor, Abba Kabir Yusuf, has announced plans to establish Cottage Industry and Cottage Enterprise Hubs across strategic locations in the state as part of efforts to accelerate industrialisation, promote local production and create employment opportunities. The governor made the announc
